_comment: Golden for DFIR Madness Case 001 - 'The Stolen Szechuan Sauce' (disk + memory + pcap, 2 Windows hosts).
corroborated by the DFIR Madness case intro, iblue.team, g4rud4/GitLab, and Netresec walkthroughs.
PROVISIONAL / HELD-OUT: VERDICT has NOT yet run this case (needs the ~16GB evidence staged +
SIFT for the disk half). min_recall_percent below is a placeholder floor to be recalibrated after
the first real scripts/benchmark run; treat this as a held-out benchmark (Phase 6). We enumerate WHAT
a correct multi-class triage must surface; finding_id -> tool_call_id mapping is populated at run time.
Fidelity caveats honored (kept generic to avoid over-assertion):
- malware is 'coreupdate.exe'/'coreupdater.exe' (same Meterpreter binary, both spellings are one IOC).
- The SHA-256 is community-walkthrough-sourced, NOT in the official key; not asserted as a match value.
- The exact registry Run-key path is inconsistent across walkthroughs; the hint stays generic.
- The prefetch execution artifact (corroboration, not in the official key) is intentionally omitted.
- Timestamps are UTC (host local is UTC-6).
license: DFIR Madness - free for education (redistribute URL + hash, not the image)

findings.description: RDP brute force against the domain controller from attacker host 194.61.24.102 (burst of failed logons then a success as CITADEL\Administrator)
findings.artifact_hint: Security.evtx burst of EID 4625 from 194.61.24.102 followed by EID 4624 for Administrator

findings.description: Interactive RDP foothold on the DC as Administrator immediately after the brute force succeeded, from 194.61.24.102
findings.artifact_hint: Security.evtx EID 4624 Logon Type 10 (RemoteInteractive) from 194.61.24.102; RDP tcp/3389 session in the PCAP

findings.description: Meterpreter payload coreupdate.exe downloaded to the DC over HTTP from 194.61.24.102
findings.artifact_hint: PCAP HTTP GET of coreupdate.exe from 194.61.24.102; corroborated by IE WebCache/browser history on disk

findings.description: Malware written to C:\Windows\System32\coreupdate.exe, masquerading as a Windows update binary
findings.artifact_hint: MFT $STANDARD_INFORMATION create time for C:\Windows\System32\coreupdate(r).exe (Meterpreter binary)

findings.description: Meterpreter reverse_tcp command-and-control beacon from the DC outbound to 203.78.103.109 on port 443
findings.artifact_hint: PCAP established TCP 10.42.85.10 -> 203.78.103.109:443; corroborated by netstat in the DC memory image

findings.description: Process injection / migration of the Meterpreter session into spoolsv.exe on the DC to hide the C2
findings.artifact_hint: Volatility malfind flags injected RWX in spoolsv.exe on the DC memory image; that PID owns the 203.78.103.109 connection

findings.description: Windows service persistence installed on the DC by the malware
findings.artifact_hint: System.evtx EID 7045 (service install); corroborated by the SYSTEM CurrentControlSet Services hive

findings.description: Registry Run-key persistence holding a base64-encoded PowerShell shellcode injector
findings.artifact_hint: A CurrentVersion\Run value containing base64 PowerShell (VirtualAlloc/CreateThread); exact hive path left generic

findings.description: Credential access - additional local account credentials recovered from the compromised DC
findings.artifact_hint: Credentials (Administrator, ricksanchez) recovered from the DC memory image via lsass / Meterpreter

findings.description: Lateral movement from the DC to the desktop DESKTOP-SDN1RPT (10.42.85.115) over RDP
findings.artifact_hint: Desktop Security.evtx EID 4624 Type 10 sourced from the DC 10.42.85.10; RDP traffic DC->Desktop in the PCAP

findings.description: Re-infection of the desktop with the same Meterpreter malware plus service persistence, beaconing to the same C2 203.78.103.109
findings.artifact_hint: Desktop System.evtx EID 7045; MFT entry for coreupdate(r).exe in System32; PCAP/memory C2 to 203.78.103.109

findings.description: Collection and staging - stolen data archived into ZIPs (secret.zip on the DC, loot.zip on the desktop)
findings.artifact_hint: MFT / filesystem entries for secret.zip (DC) and loot.zip (desktop); recipe file MACB times

findings.description: Exfiltration of the staged archives over the Meterpreter C2 channel to the attacker
findings.artifact_hint: Outbound data transfer over the Meterpreter session to 203.78.103.109; timing corroborated by the ZIP create times
